type: CLAIM

type: STATUS
files: src/PIL/Image.py
summary: Adding explicit backup/backup_suffix kwargs to Image.save() signature around line 2469-2591; will pop them before encoderinfo build and copy existing file before opening for write. Should not conflict with your dry_run as long as we both use explicit kwargs and pop before params merge - will keep edits minimal and rebase-friendly.

type: INTENT
files: src/PIL/Image.py (save() method body, lines ~2469-2591), Tests/test_image.py
summary: Implementing dry_run: params.pop('dry_run'), when true use io.BytesIO() instead of opening real file, and skip fp.close()/file creation; guard checks with 'and not dry_run'. Will keep edits localized to the open_fp/created block so your backup logic before file-open should still slot in fine - let me know if you want a specific merge order.

type: STATUS
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
summary: dry_run implementation complete: pops dry_run before encoderinfo merge, uses io.BytesIO() when true instead of opening the real file, skips fp.close()/file-creation cleanup guarded with 'and not dry_run'. No conflicts expected with your backup/backup_suffix kwargs since they operate before the open call.
